rows.Name: Chronic Hypertension
Diagnostics: Requires multiple findings over several weeks
Basic Met Panel (K Na BUN Creat)
rows.Clinical Manifestations: Signs appear very late and indicate organ damage
rows.Treatment (loop diuretic): furosemide - inhibits reabsorption in the loop of henle (H20 Na and K secretion) - first choice for renal failure
rows.Treatment (Thiazide diuretic): hydrochlorothiazide - inhibits absorption in the distal convoluted tubule (H20 Na and K secretion)
rows.Treatment (K Sparing diuretic): spironolactone - blocks aldostorone in the distal tubule - H20 and Na secreted, K spared.
rows.Treatment (ACEinhibitors): “prils”- inhibs conversion of Ang 1 to 2, prevents vasoconstrict, reduces aldosterone - side effect - cough, hyperkalemia
rows.Treatment (ARBs): “Sartans” - prevents Ang 2 from binding to receptors weaker effect that ACEinhibs and no cough, lower hyperkalemia risk, but risk for angioedema and renal fail
rows.Treatment (B blockers): “olols” - block β andrenergic receptors (1 heart, 2 lungs, can be selective or not), reduce cardiac output
rows.Treatment (CCBs ): “ipines” or diltiazem / verapamil - block movement of Ca into cells, vasodilation
rows.Treatment (\u03912 agonist ): Clonidine - lowers CO SVR and BP via lowering NE via the negative feedback receptor - sides dry mouth impotent sleep disturb, avoid sudden withdrawal

rows.Name: Ischemic Stroke
rows.Description: Partial or complete occlusion of a cerebral blood vessel, more common type
Risk Factors (nonmodifiable): Risk increases with age, doubling after 55 (2/3rds of all strokes occur in people over 65)
Risk Factors (modifiable): Heart disease - afib, mi, valve abnormalities, congenital defects
Pathophysiology (thrombolic): Thrombus forms in diseased or narrowed blood vessel in the brain
30 - 50% of the time preceded by a TIA
Pathophysiology (embolic): Embolus, typically formed in the heart (afib) lodges in a cerebral artery
onset usually sudden with severe neuro deficts
rows.Clinical Manifestations: Usually DO NOT have decreased LOC unless in brainstem

rows.Name: Transient Ischemic Attack
Description: Temporary neurologic dysfunction from a brief interruption in cerebral blood flow
Easy to ignore as symptoms are mild or resolve within 60 minutes
rows.Etiology: Can be caused by micro-emboli or plaque that temporarily blocks an artery
Diagnostics: Patients are now at a higher risk for stroke, requiring additional assessments
MRI to assess carotid and cerebral arteries and brain tissues
Clinical Manifestations: Visual deficits – blurred or double vision, tunnel vision or blindness in one eye
Motor deficits – weakness in arms, legs and hands, facial droop and ataxia
Sensory perception – numbness (hands, face, arms, or legs); vertigo
Assessment: Vital signs, ABCs, focused neurological assessment
Complete neurological workup along with an ECG and CT scan
Management: promote healthy lifestyle - smoking cessation, heart healthy, active lifestyle

rows.Name: Brain Damage
Brain damage (right): Tends to deny or minimize problems
Brain damage (left): Impaired comprehension related to language, math
